title: Astragalus cruckshanksii
intro: Astragalus cruckshanksii, also known as New Zealand milkvetch, is a perennial legume native to New Zealand and is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine. It is valued for its adaptogenic properties, which help to boost the immune system and reduce stress, and is also reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus cruckshanksii include flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Astragalus cruckshanksii include capsules, tablets, and teas, as well as topical creams and salves used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
taxonomy: Astragalus cruckshanksii is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically, it belongs to the family Fabaceae. This plant is further classified under the order Fabales, followed by the genus Astragalus and finally, the species Astragalus cruckshanksii. The classification of Astragalus cruckshanksii is also often represented using the binomial nomenclature, which is a scientific naming system. Astragalus cruckshanksii is a legume species, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family. The classification of Astragalus cruckshanksii is used in botany and taxonomy to identify and distinguish this plant from other species.
uses: Astragalus cruckshanksii, also known as Crutch's milkvetch, has been traditionally used in medicinal applications due to its potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Its root extracts have been studied for their ability to exhibit immunomodulatory effects, which may help in managing autoimmune diseases. Additionally, the plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine to support the immune system and reduce stress. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential health benefits. Studies have also explored its antioxidant and free radical scavenging activities, which could contribute to its potential therapeutic properties.

parts: Astragalus cruckshanksii is a plant species native to New Zealand, and its medicinal properties are mainly attributed to its roots. The roots of Astragalus cruckshanksii are used in traditional Maori medicine for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. They are also believed to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. The plant's roots have been used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis and infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Astragalus cruckshanksii as a medicinal plant.
preparations: Astragalus cruckshanksii, a plant native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand, has been traditionally used in herbal preparations to support immune function and overall well-being. The root of the plant is commonly harvested and dried for medicinal use. In traditional Maori medicine, it is believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. side_effects: Astragalus cruckshanksii is a plant species that may cause interactions or side effects when used in conjunction with certain medications.
